title: Song: �The Winds, As At Their Hour Of Birth
content:
The winds, as at their hour of birth,
Leaning upon the ridged sea,
Breathed low around the rolling earth
With mellow preludes, �We are free.�

The streams, through many a lilied row
Down-carolling to the crisped sea,
Low-tinkled with a bell-like flow
Atween the blossoms, �We are free.�
meaning:
This evocative piece by Alfred Lord Tennyson, titled "Song: �The Winds, As At Their Hour Of Birth", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
